zh.lifeSummary: 當Milicent de Sutton於1207年出生在英國素可巴恩哈姆時，她的父親Sir. Richard Sutton29歲，母親Mrs. Richard Sutton24歲。她於1226年12月24日與Sir Stephen Holm結婚。他們至少是4個兒子的父母。她去世於大約1248年之後在英國英格蘭。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Milicent de Sutton于1207年出生在英国素可巴恩哈姆时，她的父亲Sir. Richard Sutton29岁，母亲Mrs. Richard Sutton24岁。她于1226年12月24日与Sir Stephen Holm结婚。他们至少是4个儿子的父母。她去世于大约1248年之后在英国英格兰。

nameMeanings.name: Sutton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places called Sutton, named with Old English sūth ‘south, southern’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. The English surname is also common in Ireland (Wexford, Kildare), where it has been established since the 13th century and Gaelicized as de Sutún.
Jewish (from the former Ottoman Empire, including Syria): unexplained.
English: topographic name from Middle English (bi) suthentune ‘(at the place to the) south of the village’.

variants.name: Setton
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Avi, Ronit, Yossef.  French Andree, Henri, Jacques, Maryse.
Jewish (from the Ottoman Empire): altered form of   Sutton  .

nameMeanings.name: Milicent
meanings: A female name, which in Latin is found written Melicentia, Melissa, and Mellitta. Mr. Arthur makes Melicent, Milicent, a French name; and he translates it 'honey-sweet.' Melissa was the name of the daughter of Melissus, king of Crete, and sister of Amalthaea, who nursed Jupiter with goat's milk. She was the first that found out the making of honey, which gave the poets occasion to feign her to be turned into a bee.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Milicent de Sutton was born in 1207, in Barnham, Sussex, England, her father, Sir. Richard Sutton, was 29 and her mother, Mrs. Richard Sutton, was 24. She married Sir Stephen Holm on 24 December 1226. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. She died after about 1248, in England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Milicent de Sutton est née en 1207, à Barnham, Sussex, Angleterre, son père, Sir. Richard Sutton, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Mrs. Richard Sutton, avait 24 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Stephen Holm le 24 décembre 1226. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils. Elle est décédée après environ 1248, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Milicent de Sutton 1207 in Barnham, Sussex, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir. Richard Sutton 29 und ihre Mutter Mrs. Richard Sutton 24. Sie hat am 24. Dezember 1226 Sir Stephen Holm geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne. Sie starb nach um 1248 in England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Milicent de Sutton è nata il nel 1207 nel Barnham, Sussex, England, sua padre, Sir. Richard Sutton, aveva 29 anni e sua madre, Mrs. Richard Sutton, aveva 24 anni. Lei sposato Sir Stephen Holm il 24 dicembre 1226. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli. Morì dopo il circa 1248, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ko.lifeSummary: Milicent de Sutton(이)가 1207년 영국 수세스 바른함에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir. Richard Sutton(은)는 29세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mrs. Richard Sutton(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 1226년 12월 24일 Sir Stephen Holm(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1248년 이후 영국 잉글랜드에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Milicent de Sutton nasceu em 1207, em Barnham, Sussex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir. Richard Sutton, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Mrs. Richard Sutton, tinha 24 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Stephen Holm em 24 de dezembro de 1226. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos. Ela faleceu após aproximadamente 1248, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Milicent de Sutton родилась в 1207 г. в местности барнам, суссекс, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir. Richard Sutton было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs. Richard Sutton, 24. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Stephen Holm 24 декабря 1226 г. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына. Она умерла после около 1248 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Milicent de Sutton nació en 1207, en Barnham, Sussex, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir. Richard Sutton, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Mrs. Richard Sutton, tenía 24 años.
